# SFTP Connector - Full Documentation

# SFTP Connector 2.7

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for SFTP (SFTP Connector) manages secure file transfers over the Secure File
Transfer Protocol (SFTP). You can read files, list directories, create folders, copy, move, rename,
and delete files on SFTP servers with built-in file locking and matching capabilities. The connector
provides the same operations and behavior as File Connector and FTP Connector for consistent
integration patterns.

## Representation of File Attributes

Mule 4 provides a message structure of a payload and attributes in which the data moves around,
making that payload a file content. The attributes provide an object that contains metadata on the
payload and information such as the fileâs name, path, size, and timestamps. For FTP Connector and
SFTP Connector, these attributes are known as _file attributes_.

The Mule message contains the concepts of encoding and MIME type. These describe the format in which
a payload is represented. The file attributes describe a fileâs encoding and MIME type.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- Creating a Mule app using Anypoint Studio or Anypoint Code Builder

Before creating an app, you must have access to Anypoint Platform and Anypoint Studio.

## Next Step

After you complete the prerequisites, you are ready to create your own app and configure the
connector using [Anypoint Studio](sftp-studio).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Connect to SFTP Servers Dynamically Example

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Many integrations require connecting to different servers based on a specific condition, such as:

- Connecting to different invoice storage servers depending on the branch that emits an invoice
- Connecting to different servers depending on an integration subject, such as in a multi-tenant use
  case

To accommodate these use cases, the Anypoint Connector for SFTP (SFTP Connector) global
configuration element supports parameter expressions that evaluate these conditions and connect to
the correct server.

The following example shows how to configure SFTP Connector to connect dynamically to multiple
servers:

1.  An HTTP **Listener** source initiates the flow by reading random content posted via HTTP.
2.  The File Connector **Read** operation loads a recipients CSV file that contains a random set of
    SFTP destinations, with columns such as `host`, `user`, and `port`.
3.  A DataWeave transformation maps the content and splits the CSV file.
4.  A **For Each** component and an SFTP **Write** operation write the contents into each of the
    SFTP destinations.

    On each **For Each** iteration, the expressions set in the SFTP global configuration element
    resolve to a different value and establish different connections to each of the servers.

To test this example, create the Mule application, and run and test the application with curl
commands:

![Connect to SFTP Servers Dynamically](_images/sftp-connection-dynamically.png)

Figure 1. Connect to SFTP Servers Dynamically flow

## Create the Mule Application

To create the Mule flow:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **HTTP > Listener**.
2.  Drag **HTTP Listener** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **HTTP Listener** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display
    Name** field.
4.  Set the **Path** field to `/multitenant`
5.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of HTTP Listener in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, set the following fields:
    - **Host**: `All Interfaces [0.0.0.0] (default)`
    - **Port**: `8081`

7.  Click **OK**.
8.  Drag a **Set Variable** component to the right of the **HTTP Listener** source.
9.  Set the **Name** field to `content` and the **Value** field to `#[payload]`.
10. Drag the File Connector **Read** operation to the right of the **Set Variable** component.
11. Set the **File Path** field to `recipients.csv`.  
    This reads a CSV file that contains a random set of SFTP destinations with columns such as
    `host`, `user`, and `port`.
12. Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of File Connector in the app.
13. Accept the default settings and click **OK**.
14. On the **MIME Type** tab of the **Read** operation, for the **MIME Type** field, select
    `application/csv`.

### Configure Additional Components and Operations

Continue creating your Mule application by adding additional components and operations:

1.  In Studio, drag a **Transform Message** component to the right of the **Read** operation.
2.  In the **Output** section of the component, add the following DataWeave code to map the columns
    of the CSV file:

    DataWeave script:

    ```dataweave
    %dw 2.0
    output application/java
    ---
    payload map using (row = $) {
       host: row.Host,
       user: row.User,
       password: row.Password}
    ```

3.  Drag a **For Each** component to the right of the **Transform Message** component.
4.  Drag an SFTP **Write** operation into the **For Each** component.  
    On each For Each iteration, the **Write** operation writes the contents into each of the SFTP
    destinations.
5.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of SFTP Write operation in the app, and set the
    following fields:
    - **Host**: `#[payload.host]`
    - **Username**: `#[payload.username]`
    - **Password**: `#[payload.password]`

6.  Click **OK**.
7.  On the configuration screen, set the **Path** field to `demo.txt`.
8.  Set the **Content** field to `payload`.
9.  Drag a **Set Payload** component to the right of the **For Each** component.
10. Set the **Value** field to `Multicast Ok`.

## Run and Test your Mule Application

After creating your Mule application, run and test it:

1.  In Studio, save your Mule app.
2.  Click the project name in **Package Explorer** and then click **Run** > **Run As** > **Mule
    Application**.
3.  Open a browser and type `http://0.0.0.0:8081/multitenant`.
    - The information posted through the HTTP Listener source is written to each SFTP site multiple
      times.

      Because the listener makes use of the
      [repeatable streams feature](../../mule-runtime/latest/streaming-about), you do not need to
      worry about consuming the stream multiple times.

    - The For Each component automatically goes through each line of the CSV file.

      When reading or listing files, you might be interested in the fileâs metadata (for example,
      the file name, full path, size, timestamp). The connector uses the Mule message attributes to
      access this information.

## XML for Connecting to SFTP Servers Dynamically

Paste this code into your Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:sftp="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sftp" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sftp http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sftp/current/mule-sftp.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<file:config name="File_Config" doc:name="File Config"  />
	<sftp:config name="SFTP_Config" doc:name="SFTP Config"  >
		<sftp:connection host="#[payload.host]" username="#[payload.user]" password="#[payload.password]" />
	</sftp:config>
	<flow name="SFTPexample" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/multitenant"/>
		<set-variable value="#[payload]" doc:name="Set Variable" variableName="content"/>
		<file:read doc:name="Read" config-ref="File_Config" path="recipients.csv" outputMimeType="application/csv"/>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" >
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/java
---
payload map using (row = $) {
   host: row.Host,
   user: row.User,
   password: row.Password}]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<foreach doc:name="For Each" >
			<sftp:write doc:name="Write" config-ref="SFTP_Config" path="demo.txt">
			</sftp:write>
		</foreach>
		<set-payload value="Multicast OK" doc:name="Set Payload" />
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## See Also

- [SFTP Connector Examples](sftp-examples)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Connect to an SFTP Server Example

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following examples show how to configure Anypoint Connector for SFTP (SFTP Connector) global
configuration element to:

- [Connect to an SFTP Server](#connect-sftp-server)
- [Connect to an SFTP Server Through a Proxy](#connect-sftp-server-proxy)
- [Configure a Public Key to connect to an SFTP server](#configure-public-key)

## Connect to an SFTP Server

To configure the global element to connect to an SFTP Server, follow these steps:

1.  Select the name of the connector in the Studio canvas.
2.  Select the operation in the Studio canvas.
3.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
4.  On the **General** tab, specify the connection information for the connector.
5.  If you want EdDSA support, provide the EdDSA dependency by clicking **Configure** under the
    **Required Libraries** field.
6.  Set the **Working Directory** field to `${workingDir}`.  
    This is root of every relative path used with this connector. If itâs not provided, the value
    defaults to the remote server.
7.  Set the **Host** field to specify the SFTP server host, for example `localhost` or
    `192.168.0.1`.
8.  Set the **Port** field to `${sftpPort}`.
9.  Set the **Username** field to `anonymous` and the **Password** field to `password`.
10. On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify timeout configuration and reconnection strategy.
11. Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
12. Click **OK**.

![SFTP Connector Global Element Configuration](_images/sftp-global-configuration-with-required-lib.png)

In the Configuration XML editor, the configuration `sftp:config` looks like this:

```xml
<sftp:config name="sftp">
  <sftp:connection username="anonymous" password="password"
    host="localhost" port="${sftpPort}"
    workingDir="${workingDir}"/>
</sftp:config>
```

## Connect to an SFTP Server Through a Proxy

To configure the global element to connect to an SFTP server through a proxy, follow these steps:

1.  Select the name of the connector in the Studio canvas.
2.  Select the operation in the Studio canvas.
3.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
4.  On the **General** tab, specify the connection information for the connector.
5.  Set the **Working Directory** field to `${workingDir}`.  
    This is root of every relative path used with this connector. If itâs not provided, the value
    defaults to the remote server.
6.  Set the **Host** field to `127.0.0.100`.
7.  Set the **Port** field to `${SFTP_PORT}`.
8.  Set the **Username** field to `muletest1` and the **Password** field to `password`.
9.  For the **Sftp proxy config** field, select **Edit inline**, and set the following fields:
    - **Host**: `localhost`
    - **Port**: `${proxyPort}`
    - **Protocol**: `HTTP`  
      The protocol can be either `HTTP` or `SOCKS5`.

10. On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify a timeout configuration and reconnection strategy.
11. Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
12. Click **OK**.

![SFTP Connector Global Element Configuration](_images/sftp-global-configuration-proxy.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the configuration `sftp:config` looks like this:

```xml
<sftp:config name="sftp">
  <sftp:connection username="muletest1" password="password"
    host="127.0.0.100" port="${SFTP_PORT}" workingDir="${workingDir}">
      <sftp:sftp-proxy-config host="localhost" port="${proxyPort}" protocol="HTTP"/>
  </ftp:sftp-connection>
</ftp:config>
```

## Configure a Public Key to Connect to an SFTP Server

The following example shows how to configure a public key as the preferred authentication method in
your SFTP configuration. The example uses a PEM (Privacy Enhanced Mail) format certificate.

> [!IMPORTANT] Avoid storing private keys in your source code repository. For production, use
> [secure configuration properties](../../mule-runtime/latest/secure-configuration-properties) or a
> secrets manager.

1.  In Studio, navigate to the **Global Elements** tab.
2.  Click **Create**.
3.  Search for `sftp`.
4.  Select **SFTP Config**.
5.  Click **OK**.
6.  In the Global Element Properties window, set **Preferred authentication methods** to **Edit
    inline**.
7.  Click the plus sign to add a new value.
8.  In the **Preferred authentication method** window, set **Value** to `PUBLIC_KEY`.
9.  Click the plus sign to add a new value.
10. Set **Value** to `PASSWORD`.
11. Set **Host** to `sftp.host.com`.
12. Set **Port** to `22`.
13. Set **Username** to `sftp username`.
14. Set **Password** to `sftp password`.
15. Set **Identity file** to reference your PEM file using the `${app.home}` property, for example
    `${app.home}/keys/private.pem`. Place the PEM file in your projectâs `src/main/resources/keys`
    folder.
16. Set **Passphrase** to the password of the identity file.
17. Click **OK**.

![SFTP Connector Global Element Configuration for public key](_images/sftp-global-configuration-key.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the configuration looks like this:

```xml
<sftp:config name="SFTP_Config" doc:name="SFTP Config" >
		<sftp:connection host="sftp.host.com" username="sftp username" password="sftp password" identityFile="${app.home}/keys/private.pem" passphrase="passwordfile" >
			<sftp:preferred-authentication-methods >
				<sftp:preferred-authentication-method value="PUBLIC_KEY" />
				<sftp:preferred-authentication-method value="PASSWORD" />
			</sftp:preferred-authentication-methods>
		</sftp:connection>
	</sftp:config>
```

## See Also

- [SFTP Connector Examples](sftp-examples)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Troubleshooting SFTP Connector

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To troubleshoot Anypoint Connector for SFTP (SFTP Connector), become familiar with the information
about troubleshooting connectivity, permissions, and server-side issues, troubleshooting Mule
application issues, interpreting commonly thrown messages, and troubleshooting identity file format
issues.

## Troubleshoot Connectivity, Permissions and Server-Side Issues

Sometimes when attempting to connect to an SFTP server you can experience problems establishing the
connection because an SFTP server requires a configured encrypted communication channel. For
example, you might need to enable specific encryption cyphers or key exchange algorithms, or
indicate the location of certain authorization keys.

To resolve this issue:

1.  Locate the SFTP servers configuration file to review the parameters and settings regarding the
    connection, encryption methods, timeouts, and so forth. Correct configuration as necessary and
    customize the environment to suit your needs. For example, if you are using OpenSSH you can
    access the `sshd_config` file.
2.  Use the configuration file to increase the logging level on the server side.

## Troubleshoot Mule Application Issues

Sometimes you are not sure why the application is failing and you require a better understanding of
what is going on in the appâs interaction with the SFTP server.

To resolve this error:

- Use the configuration file to increase the logging level on the server side.
- Try to replicate the issue using an SFTP client, for example, Cyberduck or Filezilla. If the issue
  also occurs with these clients, then it is likely that the issue is not related to the Mule app.
- Get better and more detailed logs on the Mule app side by enabling verbose logging for SFTP
  Connector:
  1.  Access Anypoint Studio and navigate to the **Package Explorer** view.
  2.  Open your applicationâs project name.
  3.  Open the `src/main/resources` path folder.
  4.  Open the `log4j2.xml` file inside the folder.
  5.  If the following line is already in the `log4j2.xml` file, uncomment it to enable it;
      otherwise, add it:

      ```xml
          <AsyncLogger name="org.mule.extension.sftp" level="DEBUG"/>
      ```

  6.  To ensure the correlation ID is passed to child threads and visible in SSH logs, include the
      following argument in your run configuration:

      \-Dlog4j2.isThreadContextMapInheritable=true

- Try to minimize or remove unnecessary DataWeave data transformations (you could even use hard
  coded data) to minimize appâs complexity and determine whether the issue is with the interaction
  between the app and the SFTP server.

To get further information about enabling verbose logging for SDK components in your Mule
application, see the [Troubleshooting SDK components](../../mule-sdk/latest/troubleshooting)
documentation.

## Enable Apache SSHD Library Logging

> [!NOTE] Enabling TRACE level logging for `org.apache.sshd` generates a high volume of debug logs
> that can cause severe performance degradation, including high CPU usage, thread contention, and
> system-wide delays.

1.  Access Anypoint Studio and navigate to the **Package Explorer** view.
2.  Open your applicationâs project name.
3.  Open the `src/main/resources` path folder.
4.  Open the `log4j2.xml` file inside the folder.
5.  Add this line to enable Apache SSHD library logging:

    ```xml
        <AsyncLogger name="org.apache.sshd" level="TRACE"/>
    ```

## Enable SSL/TLS Handshake Debugging

For diagnosing SSL/TLS handshake issues, add this argument to your run configuration:

`-M-Djavax.net.debug=ssl,handshake`

This argument enables detailed SSL/TLS debugging output. Use the argument only when troubleshooting
encryption-related connection issues.

## Understand Common Throws

Here is a list of common throws messages and how to interpret them.

- SFTP:ILLEGAL_PATH

  The specified path is invalid because, for example, it does not exist.

- SFTP:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS

  An attempt was made to create a file at the same location and with the same name as an existing
  file. This happens, for example, when attempting to write a file using the CREATE_NEW write mode
  to the path of an already existing file.

- SFTP:ACCESS_DENIED

  An operation made an attempt to access an SFTP server path and perform an action for which the
  user has not been granted permission.

- SFTP:FILE_LOCK

  An operation made an unsuccessful attempt to acquire a lock over a file.

- SFTP:ILLEGAL_CONTENT

  The \*Write\* operation received invalid content to write.

- SFTP:CONNECTIVITY

  The connection to the SFTP server was interrupted.

- SFTP:INVALID_CREDENTIALS

  Authentication against the SFTP server failed due to invalid credentials.

- SFTP:CONNECTION_TIMEOUT

  The attempt to connect to the SFTP server timed out.

- SFTP:CANNOT_REACH

  An error occurred while attempting to establish the connection to the indicated SFTP server host
  and port. Typically, the connection is refused remotely, for example, if no process is listening
  on the remote address or port.

- SFTP:UNKNOWN_HOST

  Mule cannot determine the IP address of a host.

- SFTP:DISCONNECTED

  The app made too many failed login attempts to a server. This can result from multiple causes, but
  the most common one is invalid credentials.

- SFTP:KEY_EXCHANGE_FAILED

  The app tried to make an SSH connection to the server, but failed in the key exchange step.
  Failure may occur during the negotiation or later steps in the key exchange.

- FILE_DOESNT_EXIST

  The file to write was deleted.

## Troubleshoot SFTP Connector Issues with a PPK Identity File

When using the SFTP Connector version 2.0.0 and later with the preferred authentication method set
to `PUBLIC_KEY`, you can face issues if the `Identity` file is PPK. The `Apache Mina library`
doesnât support the PPK file format, so the application fails.

To resolve this issue:

1.  Convert the PPK `Identity` file to PEM by using `PuTTYgen`.
2.  Update the configuration file to use the PEM file.

    ```xml
        <sftp:config name="SFTP_Config" doc:name="SFTP Config" doc:id="34cf9b32-d3de-4171-b6e9-36dc0b25e7c0">
          <sftp:connection host="${host}" username="${user}" identityFile="${app.home}/${file-pem}" passphrase="${pwd}" port="${port}" workingDir="${root-folder}" preferredAuthenticationMethods="#[['PUBLIC_KEY']]">
          </sftp:connection>
        </sftp:config>
    ```

## Standard Append Mode Overwrites or Fails

When using the `APPEND` write mode, SFTP Connector overwrites the existing file instead of appending
data to it, or the operation fails with a generic error. This behavior occurs when the target SFTP
server is a legacy system that doesnât support the `SSH_FXF_APPEND` flag. Without this flag, the
server canât natively process a request to append data to an existing file.

To resolve this issue:

1.  In the **Write** operation configuration, set the **Write Strategy** field to `CUSTOM`.
2.  Set the **Write Mode** field to `CUSTOM_APPEND`.
3.  Adjust the **Buffer Size for Write Strategy** field. When using `CUSTOM_APPEND`, this field
    defines the size of the data chunks sent to the server. For legacy servers with high latency or
    restricted packet sizes, adjusting this value can significantly improve performance and
    stability.

> [!NOTE] The `CUSTOM_APPEND` strategy manually calculates the file offset to ensure data is added
> to the end of the file. Use this strategy only for legacy servers that donât support the
> `STANDARD` strategy, as it doesnât support concurrent writes to the same file.

## See Also

- [SFTP Connector Reference](sftp-documentation)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
- [Known Issues](https://help.salesforce.com/s/issues)
---

# Upgrade and Migrate SFTP Connector to Version 2.x

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Upgrade Anypoint Connector for SFTP (SFTP Connector) to version 2.x.x.

## Supported Upgrade Paths

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>From Version</th><th>To Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>1.6.0</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>2.x.x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Changes in This Release

This release contains the following changes:

- The connector has a new custom [**write strategy**](sftp-write#write-strategy) field that enables
  you to configure the strategy to write a file.
- The system property `mule.sftp.useHomeDirectory` was removed. SFTP Connector now uses the default
  home directory.
- SFTP Connector 2.x.x no longer supports the `jcraft library`. The connector now uses the
  `apache mina sshd` library.
- The **List** operation no longer fetches the content of each file. Now, the payload of each list
  item contains the complete path to the found file or directory.
- The SFTP **Regular Size** file attribute is renamed to **Regular File** because its behavior is
  not related to size. The file attribute value can be a file (`true`) or a directory (`false`).
- SFTP Connector 2.x.x does not support ciphers `3des-ctr` and `arcfour`. For more information,
  refer to [Supported Ciphers project](#supported-ciphers).
- The SFTP proxy protocol works only with HTTP and SOCKS5.
- SFTP Connector no longer uses `javax.activation` to resolve the media types of the files. The
  connector now uses `Apache Tika`, which can result in different media file interpretations.
- The connector changed the classes to manage the date from `LocalDateTime` to `ZonedDateTime`.
- The deprecated **Encoding** parameter in the configuration and **Write** operation is removed. The
  connector uses the Muleâs default encoding.

## Upgrade Prerequisites

Before you perform the upgrade, you must complete the following:

- Create a backup of your files, data, and configurations in case you need to restore to them.

## Upgrade Steps

Follow these steps to perform the upgrade:

1.  In Anypoint Studio, create a Mule project.
2.  In **Mule Palette**, click **Search in Exchange**.
3.  In **Add Dependencies to Project**, enter `SFTP` in the search field.
4.  In **Available modules**, select **SFTP Connector** and click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.
6.  Verify that the SFTP Connector `mule-sftp-connector` version is `2.x.x` in the `pom.xml` file in
    the Mule project.

Studio upgrades the connector automatically.

## Post Upgrade Steps

After you install the latest version of the connector, follow these steps to complete the upgrade:

### Verify the Upgrade

After you install the latest version of the connector, verify the upgrade:

1.  In Studio, verify that there are no errors in the **Problems** or **Console** views.
2.  Verify that there are no problems in the project `pom.xml` file.
3.  Test the connection to verify that the operations work.

## Supported Ciphers

The Apache Mina SSHD library does not support ciphers `3des-ctr` and `arcfour`. Additionally, the
library introduces `aes128-gcm`, `aes256-gcm`, and `cc20p1305_openssh` as alternatives.

> [!NOTE] For the current version of the SFTP Connector, you must stick to the Apache Mina default
> options. Refer to the
> [Apache Mina Supported Standards](https://github.com/apache/mina-sshd/blob/sshd-2.12.1/docs/standards.md#implementedavailable-support).

For details, review the following table:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>JSCH - SFTP Connector 1.6</th><th><a href="https://github.com/apache/mina-sshd/blob/sshd-2.9.2/sshd-common/src/main/java/org/apache/sshd/common/cipher/BuiltinCiphers.java">MINA SSHD</a> - SFTP Connector 2.0</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>blowfish-cbc</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>blowfish-cbc</code> (Deprecated)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>3des-cbc</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>3des-cbc</code> (Deprecated)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>3des-ctr</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not supported.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>aes128-cbc</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>aes128-cbc</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>aes128-ctr</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>aes128-ctr</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Not supported.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>aes128-gcm</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>aes192-cbc</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>aes192-cbc</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>aes192-ctr</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>aes192-ctr</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>aes256-cbc</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>aes256-cbc</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>aes256-ctr</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>aes256-ctr</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Not supported.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>aes256-gcm</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>arcfour</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Not supported.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>arcfour128</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>arcfour128</code> (Deprecated)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>arcfour256</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>arcfour256</code> (Deprecated)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Not supported.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>cc20p1305_openssh</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Other Supported Cryptographic Entities

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Kex Algorithms</th><th>Host Key Algorithms</th><th>MACs</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>curve25519-sha256</code>*</p></td><td><p><code>ecdsa-sha2-nistp256-cert-v01@openssh.com</code></p></td><td><p><code>hmac-sha2-256-etm@openssh.com</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>curve25519-sha256@libssh.org</code>*</p></td><td><p><code>ecdsa-sha2-nistp384-cert-v01@openssh.com</code></p></td><td><p><code>hmac-sha2-512-etm@openssh.com</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>curve448-sha512</code>*</p></td><td><p><code>ecdsa-sha2-nistp521-cert-v01@openssh.com</code></p></td><td><p><code>hmac-sha1-etm@openssh.com</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>ecdh-sha2-nistp521</code></p></td><td><p><code>ssh-ed25519-cert-v01@openssh.com</code></p></td><td><p><code>hmac-sha2-256</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>ecdh-sha2-nistp384</code></p></td><td><p><code>rsa-sha2-512-cert-v01@openssh.com</code></p></td><td><p><code>hmac-sha2-512</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>ecdh-sha2-nistp256</code></p></td><td><p><code>rsa-sha2-256-cert-v01@openssh.com</code></p></td><td><p><code>hmac-sha1</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>diffie-hellman-group-exchange-sha256</code></p></td><td><p><code>ecdsa-sha2-nistp256</code></p></td><td><p><code>hmac-md5</code> (Deprecated)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>diffie-hellman-group18-sha512</code></p></td><td><p><code>ecdsa-sha2-nistp384</code></p></td><td><p><code>hmac-md5-96</code> (Deprecated)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>diffie-hellman-group17-sha512</code></p></td><td><p><code>ecdsa-sha2-nistp521</code></p></td><td><p><code>hmac-sha1-96</code> (Deprecated)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>diffie-hellman-group16-sha512</code></p></td><td><p><code>ssh-ed25519</code>**</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>diffie-hellman-group15-sha512</code></p></td><td><p><code>sk-ecdsa-sha2-nistp256@openssh.com</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>diffie-hellman-group14-sha256</code></p></td><td><p><code>sk-ssh-ed25519@openssh.com</code>**</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>diffie-hellman-group1-sha1</code> (Deprecated)</p></td><td><p><code>rsa-sha2-512</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>diffie-hellman-group14-sha1</code> (Deprecated)</p></td><td><p><code>rsa-sha2-256</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>diffie-hellman-group-exchange-sha1</code> (Deprecated)</p></td><td><p><code>ssh-rsa</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p><code>ssh-dss</code> (Deprecated)</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p><code>ssh-dss-cert-v01@openssh.com</code> (Deprecated)</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p><code>ssh-rsa-cert-v01@openssh.com</code> (Deprecated)</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

`*` These algorithms require Bouncy Castle on Java versions before Java 11.

`**` These algorithms require externally EdDSA (Edwards-curve Digital Signature Algorithm) library.

## Troubleshooting

### Reverting the Upgrade

If you need to revert to the previous version of SFTP Connector, go to Anypoint Studio and in the
projectâs `pom.xml` file, change the `mule-sftp-connector` dependency version 2.x.x to the previous
version.

### Override SSH Connection Security Configuration

If you face SSH connection issues due to unsupported ciphers, macs, hostKeyAlgorithms or
kexAlgorithms, then change the priority or enable deprecated ciphers and algorithms forcefully.
Although using this method may introduce additional risk. Refer to
[Configure Security Parameters of SSH Connection](sftp-security-configuration-override) and
[SFTP Connector Reference](sftp-documentation).

### Override Standard Write Strategy

If your **File Write** operation overrides instead of appending when using the `APPEND` mode, use
the `CUSTOM` write strategy along with the `CUSTOM_APPEND` mode only as it isnât applicable to the
other modes. With this strategy, you can manually specify the offset for the server to write the
file in `APPEND` mode, particularly only for older SFTP servers that donât directly support write
modes. Use this strategy only if the `STANDARD` strategy results in errors during file writing in
`APPEND` mode and doesnât offer concurrency. Additionally, you can adjust the buffer size in the
**Buffer Size for Write Strategy** field.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Copy and Move Files with the SFTP Connector Example

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for SFTP (SFTP Connector) **Copy** and **Move** operations enable you to copy and
move files or directories on demand.

While SFTP has a native concept of copying and moving, FTP does not. FTP Connector simulates these
operations by downloading file content and writing it to a new location, using concurrent streams
that start writing and reading at the same time. Because these operations create significant network
overhead, use them wisely. Note that the SFTP Connector **Move** operation does not impact network
overhead.

## Configure the Copy Operation in Studio

To add and configure the **Copy** operation in Studio, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `sftp` and select the **Copy** operation.
2.  Drag the **Copy** operation onto the Studio canvas.
3.  In the **General** tab of the operation configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next
    to the **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
4.  Specify the connection information and click **OK**.
5.  Set the **Source path** field to the path of the file to copy, for example, `source.txt`.
6.  In the **Target path** field, enter the directory path into which to copy the file, for example,
    `backup`.
7.  Set the **Rename to** field to rename the file as part of the operation, for example,
    `renamed.txt`.  
    This field must be a filename, not a path. If this attribute is not provided, the original
    filename is used.

![Copy operation configuration in Studio](_images/sftp-copy-operation.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the `<sftp:copy>` configuration looks like this:

```xml
<sftp:copy sourcePath="source.txt"
  targetPath="backup" overwrite="true|false"
  createParentDirectories="true|false"
  renameTo="renamed.txt"/>
```

## Configure the Move Operation in Studio

To add and configure the **Move** operation in Studio, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `sftp` and select the **Move** operation.
2.  Drag the **Move** operation onto the Studio canvas.
3.  In the **General** tab of the operation configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next
    to the **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
4.  Specify the connection information and click **OK**.
5.  Set the **Source path** field to the path of the file to move, for example, `source.txt`.
6.  In the **Target path** field, enter the directory path into which to move the file, for example
    `backup`.
7.  Set the **Rename to** field to rename the file as part of the operation, for example,
    `renamed.txt`.  
    This field must be a filename, not a path. If this attribute is not provided, the original
    filename is used.

![Move operation configuration in Studio](_images/sftp-move-operation.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the `<sftp:move>` configuration looks like this:

```xml
<sftp:move sourcePath="source.txt"
  targetPath="backup"  overwrite="true|false"
  createParentDirectories="true|false"
  renameTo="renamed.txt"/>
```

## See Also

- [SFTP Connector Examples](sftp-examples)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Create a Directory Using SFTP Connector Example

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for SFTP (SFTP Connector) provides a **Create directory** operation that enables
you to create a directory of a given name.

If the reason for creating the directory is to immediately write, copy, or move content to the
directory, then use the **Write**, **Copy**, or **Move** operations with the **Create parent
directories** field set to **TRUE**.

## Configure the Create Directory Operation in Studio

To add and configure the **Create directory** operation in Studio, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `sftp` and select the **Create directory** operation.
2.  Drag the **Create directory** operation onto the Studio canvas.
3.  In the **General** tab of the operation configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next
    to the **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
4.  Specify the connection information and click **OK**.
5.  Set the **Directory path** field to the new directoryâs path name, for example, `~/newFolder`.

The following screenshot shows the **Create directory** operation configuration:

![Create directory operation configuration in Studio](_images/sftp-create-directory-operation.png)

Figure 1. Create directory operation configuration

In the XML editor, the `<sftp:create-directory>` configuration looks like this:

```xml
<sftp:create-directory doc:name="Create directory"  config-ref="SFTP_Config" directoryPath="~/newFolder"/>
```

## See Also

- [SFTP Connector Examples](sftp-examples)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# SFTP Connector 2.7 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for SFTP (SFTP Connector) enables secure and reliable access to files and
directories on SFTP servers. It facilitates secure file transfers between your Mule applications and
remote SFTP locations, supporting a variety of file operations such as reading, writing, and
managing files and folders.

Release Notes: [SFTP Connector Release Notes](../../release-notes/connector/connector-sftp)

> [!NOTE] PPK (Private Key) file does not support identity file for SFTP Connector.

## Configurations

---

### Default Configuration

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#config_connection">SFTP Connection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Between Size Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Wait time between size checks to determine if a file is ready to read. This allows a file write to complete before processing. If no value is provided, the check will not be performed. When enabled, Mule waits the specified time between calls and performs two size checks. If both checks return the same value, the file is ready to read. This attribute works in tandem with <strong>Time Between Size Check Unit</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Between Size Check Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A TimeUnit that qualifies the <strong>Time Between Size Check</strong> attribute.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>MILLISECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before the runtime considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the platform will expire the instance at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule runtime engine purges the instances when it sees it fit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### SFTP Connection

A file system provider that provides instances of SFTP file system from instances of SFTP Connector.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Working Directory</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The directory designated as the root of every relative path used with this connector. If not provided, it defaults to the remote server default. Define a working directory because some SFTP servers do not return output for the present working directory command. While the behavior of the SFTP server side cannot be controlled, setting this value mitigates such scenario.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Preferred Authentication Methods</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of enumeration is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>GSSAPI_WITH_MIC</p></li><li><p>PUBLIC_KEY</p></li><li><p>KEYBOARD_INTERACTIVE</p></li><li><p>PASSWORD</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of authentication methods used by the SFTP client. Valid values are: <code>GSSAPI_WITH_MIC</code>, <code>PUBLIC_KEY</code>, <code>KEYBOARD_INTERACTIVE</code>, and <code>PASSWORD</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Known Hosts File</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If provided, the client validates the serverâs key against the one in the referenced file. If the server key doesnât match the one in the file, the connection will be aborted.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sftp Proxy Config</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#SftpProxyConfig">SFTP Proxy Config</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If provided, a proxy will be used for the connection.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A scalar value representing the amount of time to wait before a connection attempt times out. This attribute works in tandem with <strong>Connection Timeout Unit</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>10</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> attribute.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A scalar value representing the amount of time to wait before an SFTP request times out. This corresponds to the internal <code>sftp-client-cmd-timeout</code> property on the <code>org.apache.sshd.sftp.client.impl.AbstractSftpClient</code> AbstractSftpClient, which is applied by <code>DefaultSftpClient</code> when sending SFTP commands. Defaults to 30 seconds (the default of the SFTP client), but can be overridden here. This attribute works in tandem with <strong>Response Timeout Unit</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>30</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A TimeUnit that qualifies the <strong>Response Timeout Unit</strong> attribute.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Idle Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the <strong>Idle Timeout</strong> attribute.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Idle Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum length of time a connection can remain inactive before the SSH client library closes it. This attribute works in tandem with <strong>Idle Timeout Unit</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The SFTP server host, such as <code>www.mulesoft.com</code>, <code>localhost</code>, or <code>192.168.0.1</code>, and so on.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The port number of the SFTP server to connect on.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>22</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username for the SFTP Server. Required if the server is authenticated.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password for the SFTP Server. Required if the server is authenticated.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Passphrase</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The passphrase (password) for the identity file if required. This parameter is ignored if identity file is not provided.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Identity File</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An identity file location for a PKI private key.<br>ssh-keygenâs OpenSSH PKI format is not accepted. You can use argument "-m PEM".</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>PRNG Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTOSELECT</p></li><li><p>NativePRNG</p></li><li><p>SHA1PRNG</p></li><li><p>NativePRNGBlocking</p></li><li><p>NativePRNGNonBlocking</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The Pseudo Random Number Generator Algorithm to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTOSELECT</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Strict Kex</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When <code>true</code>, strict key-exchange handling is enabled by default for the SSH session and is disabled automatically when the peer does not support it.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Security Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#SecurityConfiguration">Security Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Provides support to override security related configurations like Ciphers, MACs, Kex Algorithms, etc.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pooling Profile</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#PoolingProfile">Pooling Profile</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Characteristics of the connection pool.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [Copy](#copy)
- [Create Directory](#createDirectory)
- [Delete](#delete)
- [List](#list)
- [Move](#move)
- [Read](#read)
- [Rename](#rename)
- [Write](#write)

### Associated Sources

- [On New or Updated File](#listener)

## Operations

### Copy

`<sftp:copy>`

Copies the file or directory specified in **Source Path** into the **Target Path**. The source path
can be either a file or a directory. If it points to a directory, then it is copied recursively.

If the target path doesnât exist, and neither does its parent, then a parent folder is created if
**Create parent directories** is set to `true`. If **Create parent directories** is set to `false`,
then an `SFTP:ILLEGAL_PATH` error is thrown.

If **Overwrite** is set to `true` and the target file already exists, then the target file is
overwritten. Otherwise, an `SFTP:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS` error is thrown.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path to the file to copy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The target directory for where to copy the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Create Parent Directories</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether or not to attempt to create parent directories if they donât exist.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Overwrite</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether or not to overwrite the file if the target destination already exists.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Rename To</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Rename the copied file. If this value is not provided, the original file name is kept.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SFTP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFTP:ILLEGAL_PATH
- SFTP:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS
- SFTP:CONNECTIVITY

### Create Directory

`<sftp:create-directory>`

Creates a new directory using the value in the **Directory Path** field.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Directory Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The new directoryâs name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SFTP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFTP:ILLEGAL_PATH
- SFTP:ACCESS_DENIED
- SFTP:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS
- SFTP:CONNECTIVITY

### Delete

`<sftp:delete>`

Deletes the file that the path field points to, provided that the file is not locked.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path to the file to delete.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SFTP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFTP:ILLEGAL_PATH
- SFTP:ACCESS_DENIED
- SFTP:CONNECTIVITY

### List

`<sftp:list>`

Lists all the files in the **Directory Path** depending on the rules in the File Matching Rules
field.

If the listing encounters a directory, the output list will include its contents, depending on the
value of the **Recursive** field.

If **Recursive** is set to the `true` value, but a found directory is rejected by the rules in the
**File Matching Rules** field, then recursion does not occur in the directory.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Directory Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The directory path to list.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Recursive</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to include the contents of subdirectories. Defaults to <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Matching Rules</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#matcher">Matcher</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A matcher used to filter the output list.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Between Size Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Wait time between size checks to determine if a file is ready to read.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Between Size Check Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit to use in the wait time between size checks.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Message of [String] payload and [<a href="#SftpFileAttributes">SFTP File Attributes</a>] attributes. The payload contains the element path.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SFTP:ILLEGAL_PATH
- SFTP:ACCESS_DENIED
- SFTP:CONNECTIVITY
- SFTP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

### Move

`<sftp:move>`

Moves the file or directory from the **Source Path** into the **Target Path**. The source path can
be either a file or a directory. If it points to a directory, then it will be moved recursively.

If the target path doesnât exist, and neither does its parent, then a parent folder is created if
**Create parent directories** is set to `true`. If **Create parent directories** is set to `false`,
then an `SFTP:ILLEGAL_PATH` error is thrown.

If the target file already exists, then it will be overwritten if **Overwrite** is set to `true`. If
**Overwrite** is set to `false`, an `SFTP:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS` error will be thrown.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path to the file to copy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The target directory.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Create Parent Directories</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether or not to attempt to create any parent directories that donât exist.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Overwrite</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether or not to overwrite the file if the target destination already exists.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Rename To</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the moved file. If a value is not provided, the original file name is kept.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations.

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SFTP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFTP:ILLEGAL_PATH
- SFTP:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS
- SFTP:CONNECTIVITY

### Read

`<sftp:read>`

Obtains the content and metadata of a file at a given path. The operation itself returns a message
whose payload is an input stream with the fileâs content, and the metadata is represented as an
`SftpFileAttributes` object thatâs placed as the message `Message#getAttributes()` attributes.

If the lock parameter is set to `true`, then a file system level lock is placed on the file until
the input stream this operation returns is closed or fully consumed. Because the lock is provided by
the host file system, its behavior might change depending on the SFTP server or its file system.
Take that into consideration before relying on this lock.

This method also makes a best effort to determine the MIME type of the file being read by using the
fileâs extension to make an educated guess. You can also use the output **Encoding** and output
**MIME Type** optional parameters to force the encoding and MIME type.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path to the file to read.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Lock</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether or not to lock the file. Defaults to <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Between Size Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Wait time between size checks to determine if a file is ready to read.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Between Size Check Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit to use in the wait time between size checks.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure whether to use repeatable streams.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable to store the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#SftpFileAttributes">SFTP File Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SFTP:FILE_LOCK
- SFTP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFTP:ILLEGAL_PATH
- SFTP:ACCESS_DENIED
- SFTP:CONNECTIVITY

### Rename

`<sftp:rename>`

Renames the file to which the path points to the value provided in the **New Name** parameter. This
argument should not contain any path separator, or an `SFTP:ILLEGAL_PATH` error will be thrown.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path to the file to rename.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>New Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The fileâs new name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Overwrite</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether or not to overwrite the file if the target destination already exists.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SFTP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFTP:ILLEGAL_PATH
- SFTP:ACCESS_DENIED
- SFTP:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS
- SFTP:CONNECTIVITY

### Write

`<sftp:write>`

Writes the content into the file the path points to.

If the directory to which the file is attempting to be written doesnât exist, then the operation
will either throw an `SFTP:ILLEGAL_PATH` error, or create a new folder, depending on the value of
**Create parent directories**. If the file already exists, then the behavior depends on the supplied
mode.

This operation also supports locking depending on the value of the lock argument, and follows the
same rules and considerations as described in the read operation.

> [!NOTE] An additional check is performed if the file already exists, thus the FTP `STAT` command
> is executed before writing the file.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The path of the file to write.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The content to write into the file. Defaults to the current Message payload.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Create Parent Directories</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether or not to attempt to create any parent directories that donât exist.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Lock</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether or not to lock the file. Defaults to <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Write Mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>OVERWRITE</p></li><li><p>APPEND</p></li><li><p>CUSTOM_APPEND</p></li><li><p>CREATE_NEW</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A <code>FileWriteMode</code>.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>OVERWRITE</p><div><p>If the file to be written exists, then overwrite it.</p></div></li><li><p>APPEND</p><div><p>If the file to be written exists, then append the content to that file.</p></div></li><li><p>CUSTOM_APPEND</p><div><p>If the file to be written using the <code>CUSTOM</code> write strategy exists, then append the content to that file.</p></div></li><li><p>CREATE_NEW</p><div><p>Create a file and raise an error if the file exists.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>OVERWRITE</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Write Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>STANDARD</p></li><li><p>CUSTOM</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Strategy to write the file.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>STANDARD</p><div><p>The standard write function is used where the file write mode is honored.</p></div></li><li><p>CUSTOM</p><div><p>A custom write function is used to write to files where offset is manually calculated.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>STANDARD</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size For Write Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BUFFER_SIZE_1KB</p></li><li><p>BUFFER_SIZE_2KB</p></li><li><p>BUFFER_SIZE_4KB</p></li><li><p>BUFFER_SIZE_8KB</p></li><li><p>BUFFER_SIZE_16KB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Buffer size for the custom write.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>BUFFER_SIZE_8KB</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configuration name to execute the write strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] If your SFTP server is a legacy system that doesnât support the `SSH_FXF_APPEND` flag, set
> **Write Strategy** to `CUSTOM` and **Write Mode** to `CUSTOM_APPEND` to prevent files from being
> overwritten. For more information, refer to
> [Standard Append Mode Overwrites or Fails](sftp-connector-troubleshooting#standard-append-mode-overwrites-or-fails).

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

#### Throws

- SFTP:FILE_LOCK
- SFTP:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SFTP:ILLEGAL_PATH
- SFTP:ACCESS_DENIED
- SFTP:ILLEGAL_CONTENT
- SFTP:FILE_ALREADY_EXISTS
- SFTP:CONNECTIVITY
- SFTP:FILE_DOESNT_EXIST

## Sources

### On New or Updated File

`<sftp:listener>`

Polls a directory and looks for files that have been created in it. One message is generated for
each file that is found.

The key part of this functionality is how to determine that a file is actually new. There are three
strategies for that:

- Set the **Auto delete** parameter to `true` to delete each file after it is processed, which
  causes all files obtained in the next poll to be necessarily new.
- Set the **Move to directory** parameter to move each file to a different directory after it is
  processed, which achieves the same effect as **Auto delete** but without losing the file.
- Use **Watermark enabled** to pick only files that have been created or updated after the last poll
  was executed.

You can also use a matcher for additional filtering of files.

The **On New Or Updated File** source ignores files that return with an empty attributes list. The
list can be empty if the modification time (MDTM) is not enabled by the SFTP server or if the server
returns an error when listing the attributes.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Directory</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The directory in which polled files are contained.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Recursive</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether or not to also poll files contained in sub directories.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Matcher</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#matcher">Matcher</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A matcher used to filter events on files that do not meet the file matching criteria.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Watermark Enabled</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Controls whether or not to do watermarking, and if so, if the watermark should consider the fileâs modification or creation timestamps. Enable this field to have the connector pick only files that were created or updated after the completion of the latest poll.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Between Size Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Wait time (in milliseconds) between size checks to determine if a file is ready to read. This allows a file write to complete before processing. When enabled, Mule performs two size checks, waiting the specified time between calls. If both checks return the same value, the file is ready to read.<br>You can disable this feature by omitting a value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Between Size Check Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A TimeUnit which qualifies the <strong>Time between size check</strong> attribute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether this source should be executed only on the primary node when running in a cluster.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scheduling
