Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>scheduling-strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the scheduler that triggers the polling.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure whether to use repeatable streams.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors. The source stops polling if the <a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a> strategy fails. Use the <a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a> strategy to run the source indefinitely.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Auto Delete</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to delete each file after processing.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Move To Directory</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If provided, each processed file will be moved to a directory that is pointed to by this path.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Rename To</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This parameter works in tandem with <strong>Move to directory</strong>. Use this parameter to enter the name of the directory under which to move the file. Do not set this parameter if <strong>Move to directory</strong> hasnât also been set.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Apply Post Action When Failed</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether any of the post actions (<strong>Auto delete</strong> and <strong>Move to directory</strong>) should also be applied in case the file fails to be processed. If set to <code>false</code>, no failed files will be moved or deleted.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Overwrite</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables you to overwrite the target file when the destination file has the same name.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#SftpFileAttributes">SFTP File Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#config)

## Types

### SFTP Proxy Config

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Protocol</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration is of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>SOCKS5</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Security Configuration

Configuring security settings using the SSH Config Override File means you accept the
[shared responsibilities](https://docs.mulesoft.com/connectors/introduction/connectors-shared-responsibilities)
documentation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>SSH Config Override File</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the file with the SSH override configurations. It supports the following configs:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>KexAlgorithms</code><br>Specifies the comma separated list of key exchange algorithms.</p></li><li><p><code>Ciphers</code><br>Specifies the comma separated list of symmetric ciphers.</p></li><li><p><code>HostKeyAlgorithms</code><br>Specifies the comma separated list of host key signature algorithms.</p></li><li><p><code>MACs</code><br>Specifies the comma separated list of message authentication codes.</p></li></ul></div><div><p>You define the keys in the file you need to override, defaults are taken for the remaining keys.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The reconnection strategy to use. It overrides the <code>Fails Deployment</code> field. So if the reconnection strategy is set to <code>reconnect-forever</code>, the deployment never fails.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often (in milliseconds) to reconnect.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How many reconnection attempts to make.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>blocking</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></td><td><p><code>true</code></p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often (in milliseconds) to reconnect.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>blocking</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td><td><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></td><td><p><code>true</code></p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Pooling Profile

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Active</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Controls the maximum number of connections that can be active at one time. When set to a negative value, there is no limit to the number of components that can be active at one time. When <strong>Max active</strong> is exceeded, the pool is exhausted.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Controls the maximum number of connections that can sit idle in the pool at any one time. When set to a negative value, there is no limit to the number of connections that can be idle at one time.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Wait</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the number of milliseconds to wait for a pooled component to become available when the pool is exhausted and the <strong>Exhausted action</strong> is set to <code>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_WAIT</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Min Eviction Millis</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines the minimum amount of time an object can sit idle in the pool before it is eligible for eviction. When non-positive, no objects will be evicted from the pool due to idle time alone.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1800000</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Eviction Check Interval Millis</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the number of milliseconds between runs of the object evictor. When non-positive, no object evictor is executed.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>-1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Exhausted Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_GROW</p></li><li><p>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_WAIT</p></li><li><p>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_FAIL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the behavior of the Mule component pool when the pool is exhausted. Possible values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_FAIL</code>, which will throw a <code>NoSuchElementException</code>. * <code>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_WAIT</code>, which will block by invoking <code>Object.wait(long)</code> until a new or idle object is available.</p></li><li><p><code>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_GROW</code>, which will create a new Mule instance and return it, essentially making <code>maxActive</code> meaningless. If a positive <code>maxWait</code> value is supplied, it will block for, at most, the specified number of milliseconds, after which a <code>NoSuchElementException</code> will be thrown. If <code>maxThreadWait</code> is a negative value, it will block indefinitely.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_GROW</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Initialisation Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>INITIALISE_NONE</p></li><li><p>INITIALISE_ONE</p></li><li><p>INITIALISE_ALL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines how components in a pool should be initialized. The possible values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>INITIALISE_NONE, which means no components will be loaded into the pool at startup.</p></li><li><p>INITIALISE_ONE, which will load one initial component into the pool at startup.</p></li><li><p>INITIALISE_ALL, which will load all components in the pool at startup.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INITIALISE_NONE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Disabled</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether connection pooling is disabled for this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the <strong>Max idle time</strong> attribute.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### SFTP File Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Timestamp</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Regular File</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Directory</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Symbolic Link</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Permissions</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Matcher

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Timestamp Since</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Files created before this date are rejected. Any timezone specification in this value is ignored and the Mule serverâs time zone is used instead.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timestamp Until</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Files created after this date are rejected. Any timezone specification in this value is ignored and the Mule serverâs time zone is used instead.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Not Updated In The Last</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Minimum time that should pass since a file was last updated to not be rejected. This attribute works in tandem with <strong>Time unit</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Updated In The Last</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum time that should pass from when a file was last updated to not be rejected. This attribute works in tandem with <strong>Time unit</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A <strong>Not updated in the last</strong> attributes.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MILLISECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Case Sensitive</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables you to configure an external file system matcher as case sensitive or insensitive.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Filename Pattern</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A matching pattern to apply on the file name. Supports glob expressions (default) and regex expressions. To use any of the expressions set a prefix, for example, <code>glob:*.{java, js}</code> or <code>regex:[0-9]test.csv</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path Pattern</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A matching pattern to apply on the file path</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Directories</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>REQUIRE</p></li><li><p>INCLUDE</p></li><li><p>EXCLUDE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Match only if the file is a directory</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INCLUDE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Regular Files</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>REQUIRE</p></li><li><p>INCLUDE</p></li><li><p>EXCLUDE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Match only if the file is a regular file</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INCLUDE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sym Links</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>REQUIRE</p></li><li><p>INCLUDE</p></li><li><p>EXCLUDE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Match only if the file is a symbolic link</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INCLUDE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Min Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The minimum file size in bytes. Files smaller than the specified value are rejected.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum file size in bytes. Files larger than the specified value are rejected.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is the amount of memory to allocate to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can fit into this buffer, then it will be expanded according to the <strong>Buffer size increment</strong> attribute, with an upper limit of <strong>Max in memory size</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size will be expanded if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, and that a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error will be raised when the buffer gets full.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is the maximum amount of memory to use. If more than the specified maximum is used, then a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error will be raised. A value lower or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration is one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which all these attributes are expressed.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the maximum memory that the stream should use to keep data in memory. If more than that is consumed then content is buffered on the disk.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum number of times a message can be redelivered and processed unsuccessfully before triggering process-failed-message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The secure hashing algorithm to use. If not set, the default is <code>SHA-256</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Identifier</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicyMessageIdentifier">Redelivery Policy Message Identifier</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines how to identify a redelivered message for this policy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The object store where the redelivery counter for each message will be stored.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy Message Identifier

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Use Secure Hash</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines one or more expressions to use to determine when a message has been redelivered. Set this property only when <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> is <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is the number of instances initially allowed to be kept in memory in order to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can fit into this buffer, then it will be expanded according to the <strong>Buffer size increment</strong> attribute, with an upper limit of <strong>Max in memory size</strong>. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How much the buffer size will expand if it exceeds its initial specified size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, and that a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error will be raised when the buffer gets full. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max in Memory Instances</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is the maximum amount of memory to use. If more than the maximum amount is used, then a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error will be raised. A value lower or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Objects</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum number of instances to keep in memory. If more than the maximum is required, content starts to buffer to the disk.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

[Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# SFTP Connector Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To fully benefit from using Anypoint Connector for SFTP (SFTP Connector), see the following examples
of configuring the connector operations:

- [Read a File](sftp-read)  
  Configure the **Read** operation to read a file at any point in the flow.
- [Write a File](sftp-write)  
  Configure the **Write** operation to write content into the given path on demand.
- [List Files](sftp-list)  
  Configure the **List** operation to list the files and folders in the configured directory path.
- [Copy and Move Files](sftp-copy-move)  
  Configure the **Copy** and **Move** operations to copy or move files or directories on demand.
- [Create a Directory](sftp-create-directory)  
  Configure the **Create Directory** operation to create a directory of a given name.
- [Trigger a Flow for a New Created or Updated File](sftp-on-new-file)  
  Configure the **On New or Updated File** source to trigger a flow for a new created or modified
  file.
- [Connect to an SFTP Server](sftp-connection)  
  Configure the SFTP global configuration element to connect to an SFTP server, or to connect to an
  SFTP server through a proxy.
- [Connect to SFTP Servers Dynamically](sftp-connection-dynamically)  
  Configure SFTP Connector to connect dynamically to multiple servers.
- [Disable pooling in SFTP Configuration](sftp-pooling)  
  Disable connection pooling in SFTP Connector configuration to make sure no connection is active if
  there is nothing to transfer.
- [Configure Preferred Authentication](sftp-preferred-authentication)  
  Configure the preferred authentication for the SFTP Connection.
- [Configure Security Parameters of SSH Connection](sftp-security-configuration-override)  
  Override default security configuration of SSH Connection.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# List Files Using the SFTP Connector

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for SFTP (SFTP Connector) provides a **List** operation that returns a list of
messages representing files or folders in the directory path:

- The path you define in the **Directory path** field can be absolute, or it can be relative to a
  working directory.
- By default, the operation does not read or list files or folders within any subfolders of the
  directory path.
- To list files or folders within any subfolders, set the **Recursive** field to **TRUE**.

## Configure the List Operation in Studio

To add and configure the **List** operation in Studio, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `sftp` and select the **List** operation.
2.  Drag the **List** operation onto the Studio canvas.
3.  In the **General** tab of the operation configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next
    to the **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
4.  Specify the connection information and click **OK**.
5.  In the **General** tab, set the **Directory path** field to `~/dropFolder` to set the path of
    the file to list.

![List operation configuration in Studio](_images/sftp-list-operation-1.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the `<sftp:list>` configuration looks like this:

```xml
<sftp:list doc:name="List" config-ref="SFTP_Config" directoryPath="~/dropFolder"/>
```

## Examples

### Basic use case

The following XML example lists the folder contents of messages in the directory path without the
subfolder contents. The **For Each** and **Choice** components manage each directory in the list
differently from the way they manage each file:

```xml
<flow name="list">
  <sftp:list config-ref="SFTP_Config" directoryPath="~/dropFolder" />
  <foreach>
    <choice>
      <when expression="#[attributes.directory]">
        <flow-ref name="processDirectory" />
      </when>
      <otherwise>
        <logger message="Found file #[attributes.path]" />
		<sftp:read config-ref="SFTP_Config" path="#[payload]" />
		<logger message="Whose content is #[payload]" />
      </otherwise>
    </choice>
  </foreach>
</flow>
```

### Poll a Directory

You can combine a **Scheduler** source with the SFTP **List** operation to poll a directory to look
for new files to process.

> [!NOTE] For automatic polling, use the SFTP [On New or Updated File](sftp-on-new-file) operation
> which enables you to optionally set post-processing options to automatically move or delete files.

In the following poll directory example, a flow lists the contents of a folder once per second. The
flow then processes the files one by one, deleting each file after it is processed because there is
a **Delete** operation in the **For Each** component.

The following screenshot shows the flow in Studio:

![Pool a directory flow in Studio](_images/sftp-list-operation-2.png)

To create the flow, follow these steps:

1.  In Studio, drag the **Scheduler** component onto the Studio canvas.
2.  Drag the SFTP **List** operation to the right of **Scheduler**.
3.  In the **General** tab of the operation configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next
    to the **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
4.  Specify the connection information and click **OK**.
5.  In the **General** tab, set the **Directory path** field to `/config/dropFolder` to set the path
    of the file to list.
6.  Drag a **For each** component to the right of the **List** operation.
7.  Drag a **Flow Reference** component inside the **For each** component.
8.  Set the **Flow name** field to `processFile` to specify the flow reference that process the
    files.
9.  Drag an SFTP **Delete** operation to the right of the **Flow Reference** component.
10. Set the **Connector configuration** field to the previously configured connection in the
    **List** operation.
11. Set the **Path** field to `#[payload]`.
12. Drag a **Transform Message** component below the first flow.
13. Select the new flow and change the **Name** field to `processFile`.
14. Drag an SFTP **Read** operation to the left of the **Transform Message** component and set the
    **Path** field to `#[payload]`.
15. Select the **Transform Message** component in the new flow, and in the **Output** view, paste
    the following DataWeave expression:

    ```dataWeave
    %dw 2.0
    						output application/json
    						---
    						characters: payload.characters.*name map (
    						            (item, index) -> {name: item}
    						         )
    ```

16. Drag a **Logger** component to the right of **Transform Message**.
17. Set the **Message** field to `payload`.
18. Drag an Objet Store **Store** operation to the right of **Logger**.
19. Set the **Key** field to `test-file-' random() as String '.json'`, and **Value** to `payload`.
20. Save your Mule application.

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the configuration looks like this:

```xml
?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:os="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os"
    xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:ftps="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ftps"
	xmlns:sftp="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sftp"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sftp http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sftp/current/mule-sftp.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ftps http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ftps/current/mule-ftps.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os/current/mule-os.xsd">

	<sftp:config name="SFTP_Config" doc:name="SFTP Config" >
		<sftp:connection host="localhost" port="2222" username="bob" password="pass"/>
	</sftp:config>

	<os:object-store name="Object_store" doc:name="Object store" />

	<flow name="poll">
	  <scheduler>
	      <scheduling-strategy>
	          <fixed-frequency frequency="1000"/>
	      </scheduling-strategy>
	  </scheduler>

	  <sftp:list directoryPath="/config/dropFolder" config-ref="SFTP_Config"/>

	  <foreach>
	      <flow-ref name="processFile" />
	      <sftp:delete path="#[payload]" config-ref="SFTP_Config"/>
	  </foreach>
	</flow>

	  <flow name="processFile" maxConcurrency="1">
	  		<sftp:read config-ref="SFTP_Config" path="#[payload]" />
			<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" >
				<ee:message >
					<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
						output application/json
						---
						characters: payload.characters.*name map (
						            (item, index) -> {name: item}
						         )]]></ee:set-payload>
				</ee:message>
			</ee:transform>

			<logger level="ERROR" message="#[payload]" />
			<os:store doc:name="Store" objectStore="Object_store" key="#['test-file-' ++ random() as String ++ '.json']"/>
	  </flow>

</mule>
```

## Match Filter

When listing files, use the **File Matching Rules** field, which accepts files that match the
specified criteria. This field defines the possible attributes to either accept or reject a file.
These attributes are optional and ignored if you do not provide values for them. Use an `AND`
operator to combine individual attributes.

To configure the field in Studio, set the **File Matching Rules** field to **Edit inline** and
complete the desired attributes:

- **Timestamp since**  
  Files created before this date are rejected. Any timezone specification in this value is ignored
  and the Mule serverâs time zone is used instead.
- **Timestamp until**  
  Files created after this date are rejected. Any timezone specification in this value is ignored
  and the Mule serverâs time zone is used instead.
- **Not updated in the last**  
  Minimum time that should pass since a file was last updated to not be rejected. This attribute
  works in tandem with **Time unit**.
- **Updated in the last**  
  Maximum time that should pass from when a file was last updated to not be rejected. This attribute
  works in tandem with **Time unit**.
- **Time unit**  
  A **Not updated in the last** attributes. Defaults to `MILLISECONDS`.
- **Filename pattern**  
  Similar to the current filename pattern filter but more powerful. Glob expressions (default) and
  regex are supported. You can select which one to use by setting a prefix, for example,
  `glob:*.{java, js}` or `regex:[0-9]test.csv`.
- **Path pattern**  
  Same as **Filename pattern** but applies over the entire file path rather than just a filename.
- **Directories**  
  Match only if the file is a directory.
- **Regular files**  
  Match only if the file is a regular file.
- **Sym links**\`  
  Match only if the file is a symbolic link.
- **Min size**  
  Inclusive lower boundary for the file size, expressed in bytes.
- **Max size**  
  Inclusive upper boundary for the file size, expressed in bytes.

![File Matching Rules field configuration](_images/sftp-list-operation-3.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the configuration looks like this:

```xml
<sftp:matcher
  timestampSince="2019-06-03T13:21:58+00:00"
  timestampUntil="2019-06-03T13:21:58+00:00"
  timeUnit="MICROSECONDS"
  filenamePattern="a?*.{htm,html,pdf}"
  pathPattern="a?*.{htm,html,pdf}"
  directories="REQUIRE"
  regularFiles="REQUIRE"
  symLinks="REQUIRE"
  maxSize="1024"/>
```

### External File System Matcher

SFTP Connector enables you to use an external file system matcher. Configure the matcher as case
sensitive or insensitive in the **Case sensitive** field.

![Case sensitive field set to TRUE](_images/sftp-list-operation-4.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the `caseSensitive` configuration looks like this:

```xml
<sftp:list doc:name="List" config-ref="SFTP_Config" directoryPath="/upload">
	    <sftp:matcher filenamePattern="*.txt" caseSensitive="true"/>
	</sftp:list>
```

### Top-Level, Reusable Matcher

You can use the file matcher as either a named top-level element that enables reuse or as an inner
element that is proprietary to a particular component.

The following example shows a top-level reusable matcher:

```xml
<sftp:matcher name="smallFileMatcher" maxSize="100" />
      <flow name="smallFiles">
      <sftp:list path="~/smallfiles" matcher="smallFileMatcher" />
      ...
</flow>
```

### Inner Nonreusable Matcher

The following example shows an inner nonreusable matcher:

```xml
<flow name="smallFiles">
      <sftp:list path="~/smallfiles" matcher="smallFileMatcher">
            <sftp:matcher maxSize="100" />
      </sftp:list>
      ...
</flow>
```

## See Also

- [SFTP Connector Examples](sftp-examples)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Trigger a Flow When a New File Is Created or Modified Example

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connectors for File, FTP, and SFTP provide an **On New or Updated File** operation that
polls a directory for files that have been created or updated. A message is generated for each file
that is found.

The following strategies determine that a file is new:

- Set the **Auto delete** field to **True** to delete each file after it is processed, which causes
  all files obtained in the next poll to be considered new.
- Set the **Move to directory** field to move each file to a different directory after it is
  processed, which achieves the same effect as **Auto delete** but without erasing the file.
- Set the **Watermark** field to pick only files that have been created or updated after the last
  poll was executed.

You can also use a matcher for additional filtering of files.

## Configure the On New or Updated File Operation in Studio

To add and configure the **On New or Updated File** operation in Studio, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `sftp` and select the **On New or Updated File**
    operation.
2.  Drag the **On New or Updated File** operation onto the Studio canvas.
3.  In the **General** tab of the operation configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next
    to the **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
4.  Specify the connection information and click **OK**.
5.  Set the **Directory** field to the directory path on which pooled files are contained, for
    example, `test-data/in`.
6.  Set the **Auto delete** field to **True**.
7.  Select the **Watermark** field to enable watermarking.

![On New or Updated File operation configuration in Studio](_images/sftp-newupdated-operation.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the `<sftp:listener>` configuration looks like this:

```xml
<flow name="onNewFile">
    <sftp:listener config-ref="file" directory="test-data/in" autoDelete="true" watermarkEnabled="true">
      <scheduling-strategy>
        <fixed-frequency frequency="1000"/>
      </scheduling-strategy>
    </sftp:listener>

    <flow-ref name="processFile" />
</flow>
```

## See Also

- [SFTP Connector Examples](sftp-examples)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Disable Pooling in the SFTP Connector Configuration

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Disable connection pooling in the Anypoint Connector for SFTP (SFTP Connector) configuration to make
sure no connection is active if there is nothing to transfer.

1.  In Studio, navigate to the **Global Elements** tab.
2.  Select the **SFTP Connector** config.
3.  Click **Edit**.
4.  In the Global Elements properties tab, click the **Advanced** tab.
5.  Set **Pooling profile** to **Edit inline**.
6.  Set the values of **Max active**, **Max idle**, and **Max wait** to `0` to ensure that no
    connection is active if there is nothing to transfer.

![SFTP Connection pooling configuration in Studio](_images/sftp-pooling.png)

## See Also

- [SFTP Connector Examples](sftp-examples)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Configure Preferred Authentication

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for SFTP (SFTP Connector) enables you to configure preferred authentication
methods in the SFTP global configuration of the connector. The following example shows how to
configure different authentication methods in Anypoint Studio.

1.  In Studio, navigate to the **Global Elements** tab.
2.  In the Choose Global Type window, type `sftp` in the search bar.
3.  Select **SFTP Config**.
4.  Click **OK**.
5.  Set **Preferred authentication methods** to **Edit inline**.
6.  Click the plus sign to add one of the valid authentication methods: `GSSAPI_WITH_MIC`,
    `PUBLIC_KEY`, `KEYBOARD_INTERACTIVE`, or `PASSWORD`.
7.  Set **Value** to `GSSAPI_WITH_MIC`.
8.  Complete the remaining connection fields.
9.  Click **Test Connectionâ¦â**.
10. Click **OK**.

![SFTP Configuration with preferred authentication field set to GSSAPI_WITH_MIC](_images/sftp-preferred-authentication.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor window, the configuration looks like this:

```xml
<sftp:config name="SFTP_Config" doc:name="SFTP Config" >
		<sftp:connection host="localhost" >
			<sftp:preferred-authentication-methods >
				<sftp:preferred-authentication-method value="GSSAPI_WITH_MIC" />
			</sftp:preferred-authentication-methods>
		</sftp:connection>
	</sftp:config>
```

## See Also

- [SFTP Connector Examples](sftp-examples)
---

# Read a File Using the SFTP Connector Example

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for SFTP (SFTP Connector) provides a **Read** operation that reads a file at any
point in the flow. The operation reads the file in the given path and returns a Mule message with
the following attributes:

- The fileâs content as the payload
- The fileâs metadata in the message attributes
- If the file does not exist, you receive an `SFTP:ILLEGAL_PATH` error

  Note that the operation does not read directories.

## Configure the Read Operation in Studio

To add and configure the **Read** operation in Studio, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `sftp` and select the **Read** operation.
2.  Drag the **Read** operation onto the Studio canvas.
3.  In the **General** tab of the operation configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next
    to the **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
4.  Specify the connection information and click **OK**.
5.  In the **General** tab, set the **File Path** field to the path of the file to read, for
    example, `#[path]`.
6.  In the **Advanced** tab, set the **MIME Type** field to `application/xml` and the **Encoding**
    field to `ISO 10646/Unicode(UTF-8)`.

The following screenshot shows the **Read** operation configuration:

![Read operation configuration in Studio](_images/sftp-read-operation.png)

Figure 1. Read operation configuration

In the XML editor, the `<sftp:read>` configuration looks like this:

```xml
<sftp:read path="#[path]"
  lock="true|false"
  outputEncoding="UTF-8"
  outputMimeType="application/xml" />
```

## Transactions Per Second

The following table compares the total requests per second (TPS) and average success TPS for the
**Read** operation of the SFTP Connector versions:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Connector version</th><th>Mule runtime engine version</th><th>Total requests for v1.6.1</th><th>Total requests for v2.0.1</th><th>Average success TPS for v1.6.1</th><th>Average success TPS for v2.0.0</th><th>Percentage of decreased TPS performance</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>1.6.1 vs 2.0.0</p></td><td><p>4.4.1</p></td><td><p>301,377.40</p></td><td><p>247,614.40</p></td><td><p>1,674</p></td><td><p>1,375.60</p></td><td><p>-18</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>1.6.1 vs 2.0.0</p></td><td><p>4.5.0</p></td><td><p>326,915.80</p></td><td><p>247,296.20</p></td><td><p>1,816</p></td><td><p>1,374</p></td><td><p>-24</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## MIME Types and Encoding

Each connector tries to determine a fileâs MIME type from its extension. You can force the MIME type
to a different value with the **MIME Type** (`outputMimeType`) parameter.

The same process works for encoding. By default, the connector assumes that the default encoding in
the Mule runtime matches the MIME type of the file, however, you can set the encoding through the
**Encoding** (`outputEncoding`) parameter.

## Why MIME Types Matter

DataWeave expressions are embeddable inside operations that generate payloads and other values.
Having the correct MIME type set helps DataWeave auto-assign types and also generate the correct
outputs. Also, maximizing the use of DataSenseâs functionality improves the user experience.

## File Locks

File system level locks are not possible in FTP or SFTP, so each operation uses a Mule lock that
protects only the file from other flows in the same Mule application. These locks do not protect the
file from external processes. However, if the Mule application is running on a cluster, the lock is
distributed.

The file lock is automatically released when any of the following occurs:

- The Mule flow that locked the file ends
- The file content has been fully read

> [!NOTE] If the file is already locked by someone else, the connector cannot unlock it, resulting
> in a `FTP:FILE_LOCK` error.

## See Also

- [SFTP Connector Examples](sftp-examples)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Configure Security Parameters of SSH Connection

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following example shows how to configure the Anypoint Connector for SFTP (SFTP Connector) to
override security parameters using Anypoint Studio.

## Create a Configuration Override File

Follow these steps to create the file:

1.  Navigate to your `src/main/resources` folder.
2.  Create a config file, for example, `mule_sshd_config` with the following SSHD content. The file
    follows the format of an `sshd_config` file:

    ```xml
    KexAlgorithms ecdh-sha2-nistp521,ecdh-sha2-nistp384,ecdh-sha2-nistp256,diffie-hellman-group18-sha512,diffie-hellman-group17-sha512,diffie-hellman-group16-sha512,diffie-hellman-group14-sha256
    Ciphers aes128-cbc,aes128-ctr,aes192-cbc,aes192-ctr,aes256-cbc,aes256-ctr
    HostKeyAlgorithms ssh-rsa,rsa-sha2-256,rsa-sha2-512
    MACs hmac-sha1,hmac-sha2-256,hmac-sha2-512
    ```

    ![Contents of the override config file](_images/sftp-override-config-file-contents.png)

3.  Configure the keys and values accordingly. If you do not provide a key, then default values are
    used.

The following example shows the location of the config file within the `src/main/resources` folder:

![Location of the override config file](_images/sftp-override-config-file-location.png)

## Configure the Override File in Anypoint Studio

Follow these steps to configure the override file:

1.  In Studio, navigate to the **Global Elements** tab.
2.  In the Choose Global Type window, type `sftp` in the search bar.
3.  Select **SFTP Config**.
4.  Click **OK**.
5.  Go to the **Advanced** tab.
6.  Under **Security Configuration**, enter `ssh config override file`.
7.  Enter the path to the file, for example: `${app.home}/mule_sshd_config`.
8.  Complete the remaining connection fields.
9.  Click **Test Connectionâ¦â**.
10. Click **OK**.

![SFTP Configuration with preferred authentication field set to GSSAPI_WITH_MIC](_images/sftp-override-security-parameters.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor window, the configuration looks like this:

```xml
<sftp:config name="SFTP_Config" doc:name="SFTP Config" >
    <sftp:connection host="localhost" sshConfigOverride="${app.home}/mule_sshd_config">
    </sftp:connection>
</sftp:config>
```

## Verify the Override File Creation

Consider the following points when verifying the file creation:

- Search for the log `Read the config file` and verify that the filename and the configuration
  values fetched from the file are valid.
- Find ignored configs with the log line `Config keys found but ignored`.
- Check that if the connector is unable to locate the file, then it logs
  `Couldnât locate config file`.

## See Also

- [SFTP Connector Examples](sftp-examples)
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure SFTP Connector

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure a source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-input-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems in real time, as
described in [View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[SFTP Connector Reference Reference](sftp-documentation).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add SFTP Connector to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with the connectorâs
namespace and schema location and to add the required dependencies to the projectâs `pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In the **Add Dependencies to Project** window, type `sftp` in the search field.
3.  Click **SFTP Connector** in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of these sources
to use with SFTP Connector:

- **On New or Updated File**  
  Initiates a flow by polling a directory for files that have been created or updated and generates
  a message for each file found.
- **HTTP Listener**  
  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port.
- **Scheduler**  
  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met.

For example, to configure the On New or Updated File source, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **On New or Updated File**.
2.  Drag **On New or Updated File** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **On New or Updated File** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the
    **Display Name** field.
4.  Specify a value for the **Path** field.
5.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of **On New or Updated File** in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, specify the connection information for the connector.
7.  Set the **Host** field to specify the SFTP server host, for example, `localhost` or
    `192.168.0.1`.
8.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
9.  Click **OK**.
10. On the **On New or Updated File** configuration screen, specify a **Scheduling Strategy** value
    and optionally complete other fields.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you immediately define a specific operation for
that connector to perform.

To add an operation for SFTP Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **SFTP** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas and to the right of the source.

The following screenshot shows the SFTP Connector operations in the Mule Palette view of Anypoint
Studio:

![SFTP Connector Operations](_images/sftp-operations.png)

Figure 1. SFTP Connector Operations

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, itâs best to configure a global element that all instances of that
connector in the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to configure the SFTP server
connection.

To configure the global element for SFTP Connector, follow these steps:

1.  Select the name of the connector in the Studio canvas.
2.  Select the operation in the Studio canvas.
3.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
4.  On the **General** tab, specify the connection information for the connector:
5.  If you want EdDSA support, provide the EdDSA dependency by clicking **Configure** under the
    **Required Libraries** field.
6.  Set the **Working Directory** as the root of every relative path used with this connector.  
    If itâs not provided, the value defaults to the remote server.
7.  Set the **Host** field to specify the SFTP server host, for example, `localhost` or
    `192.168.0.1`.
8.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify a timeout configuration and reconnection strategy.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
10. Click **OK**.

The following screenshot shows the SFTP Connector **Global Element Configuration** window in
Anypoint Studio:

![SFTP Connector Global Element Configuration](_images/sftp-global-configuration-with-required-lib.png)

Figure 2. SFTP Connector Global Element Configuration

In the XML editor, the configuration `sftp:config` looks like this:

```xml
<sftp:config name="sftp">
  <sftp:connection username="anonymous" password="password"
    host="localhost" port="${sftpPort}"
    workingDir="${workingDir}"/>
</sftp:config>
```

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the output is visible in the Anypoint Studio
  console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log is visible in your OS
  console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio)
- [SFTP Connector Reference](sftp-documentation)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Write a File Using the SFTP Connector Example

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for SFTP (SFTP Connector) provides a **Write** operation that writes content into
the given path on demand.

## Configure the Write Operation in Studio

To add and configure the **Write** operation in Studio, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `sftp` and select the **Write** operation.
2.  Drag the **Write** operation onto the Studio canvas.
3.  In the **General** tab of the operation configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next
    to the **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
4.  Specify the connection information and click **OK**.
5.  In the **General** tab, set the **Path** field to `output.csv` to set the path of the file to
    write.  
    By default, the connector writes whatever is in the message payload.

![Write operation configuration in Studio](_images/sftp-write-operation-1.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the `<sftp:write>` configuration looks like this:

```xml
<sftp:write path="output.csv" />
```

## Use Embedded DataWeave Transformations

If you place a **Transform Message** component before the **Write** operation, the message payload
changes, and that impacts the operation that is placed after the **Write** operation. Therefore, if
the payload is in a different format and you need to transform it before writing it, you can place
the transformation inside the **Content** field of the **Write** operation to generate content to be
written without affecting the message in transit.

The following example places the DataWeave transformation in the **Content** field:

![Write operation configuration in Studio with Content field](_images/sftp-write-operation-2.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the configuration looks like this:

```xml
<sftp:write path="output.csv">
  <sftp:content>#[%dw 2.0
                   output application/csv
                   ---
                   payload.customers.email
                  ]
  </sftp:content>
</sftp:write>
```

## Write into Directories

Because the **Write** operation fails by default if you set nonexistent directories in the **Path**
field, set the **Create parent directories** field to **True (Default)** to automatically create any
missing directories.

The following example shows that if any of the `a`, `b`, or `c` directories set in the **Path**
field do not exist, the connector creates them:

![.Write operation configuration with Create parent directories](_images/sftp-write-operation-3.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the configuration looks like this:

```xml
<sftp:write path="a/b/c/myFile.txt" />
```

## Use Write Strategy

Use the **Write strategy** field to configure the strategy to write the file. You can configure the
field to select either of these strategies:

- `STANDARD`  
  This is the default strategy that you can use in the `APPEND`, `OVERWRITE`, and `CREATE_NEW`
  modes.
- `CUSTOM`  
  This strategy enables you to manually specify the offset for the server to write the file in
  `APPEND` mode, particularly only for older SFTP servers that do not directly support write modes.

Additionally, you can configure the buffer size for writing into the file via the **Buffer size for
write strategy** field.

> [!NOTE] Use the `CUSTOM` write strategy with the `CUSTOM_APPEND` mode because it isnât applicable
> to the `OVERWRITE` or `CREATE_NEW` modes. Use this strategy only if the `STANDARD` strategy
> results in errors during file writing in `APPEND` mode. Concurrency isnât maintained for servers
> that donât follow SFTP guidelines.

The following example shows the configuration for both fields:

![Write operation configuration with write strategy and buffer size for write strategy fields](_images/sftp-write-operation-4.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the configuration looks like this:

```xml
<sftp:write  path="testFile.csv" mode="APPEND" writeStrategy="CUSTOM" bufferSizeForWriteStrategy="BUFFER_SIZE_16KB" config-ref="SFTP_Config"/>
```

## Write to Existing Files

When writing content to a file, set the **Write Mode** field to any of the following modes:

- **OVERWRITE (Default)**  
  If the file exists, overwrite it completely.
- **APPEND**  
  If the file exists, add its content to the end of an existing file.
- **CREATE_NEW**  
  Create a new file. If the file already exists, then you receive an error.

## Locks

The **Write** operation supports locking similar to the **Read** operation. The main difference is
that the lock releases automatically after the **Write** operation finishes. Set the **Lock** field
to any of the following modes:

- **TRUE**
- **FALSE (Default)**

## See Also

- [SFTP Connector Examples](sftp-examples)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# SFTP Connector XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Although you can manually code a Mule app in XML, it is more efficient to use Anypoint Studio.

If you manually code a Mule runtime engine (Mule) app in XML, either from the Anypoint Studio XML
editor or from a text editor, you can get access to the connector in your app by adding reference
statements to both your XML Mule flow and the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.

When you add the connector in Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code with the
connectorâs namespace and schema location. It also adds a dependency to the `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `<mule>` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sftp
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sftp /current/mule-sftp .xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the `<mule>` XML block:

```xml
<mule xmlns:sftp ="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sftp "
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sftp
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sftp/current/mule-sftp.xsd">
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Apache Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app.
If you code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access to
this connector:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.mule.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-sftp-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `x.x.x` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `sftp connector`.
4.  Select the connector.
5.  Click **Dependency Snippets** near the upper right of the screen.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
